name: IRUSA Waqf
mission: ESTABLISH A POOL OF CAPITAL THAT ENSURES THE LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY OF IRUSA AND ENABLES THE CHARITY IN ACHIEVING ITS OBJECTIVES.
theoryOfChange: Zakat described as purification/growth/blessing and distributed according to Islamic principles; Sadaqah Jariyah described as continuous charity that supports long-term projects (water systems, clinics, schools, farms, small businesses) that benefit communities for generations.
scoreSummary: Irusa Waqf shows good impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Program expenses exceed total expenses
keyConcerns.detail: Financial data may be inconsistent across sources or reporting periods.

keyConcerns.headline: Expense components don't sum to total (>10% difference)
keyConcerns.detail: Financial data may be inconsistent across sources or reporting periods.

keyConcerns.headline: Fundraising costs are 100% of total expenses
keyConcerns.detail: High fundraising spend reduces funds available for programs.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 6.1% of revenue ($146,530 on $2,405,280)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

keyConcerns.headline: Cost per beneficiary is $0.01 — implausibly low
keyConcerns.detail: A cost below $1/beneficiary usually means beneficiary counts are inflated or double-counted.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 30/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Separate restricted/endowment funds from operating reserves and publish a clear reserve-use policy.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly)

all_citations.claim: The organization holds 392.9 months of working capital and a program expense ratio of 80.1%.
Total revenue was $2,405,280 and fundraising efficiency was $0.06 per $1 raised.
The charity operates the WAQF IRUSA Endowment to ensure long-term sustainability.
Donations to the organization support general charitable giving and religious-cultural causes.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The charity earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and stability, though its impact score is tempered by the nature of endowment-based direct service.
areas_for_improvement: Limited external data availability for third-party impact verification
Narrower program scope compared to broader humanitarian relief organizations

dimension_explanations.alignment: Irusa Waqf shows high donor fit for those seeking to contribute to long-term religious and cultural sustainability through sadaqah-eligible giving [3][4]. The alignment score is supported by its clear mission to provide permanent support for charitable objectives, though it is constrained by a lack of multi-year growth data in current reporting [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a program expense ratio of 80.1%, indicating that the majority of its $2,405,280 in total revenue is directed toward its endowment and distribution programs [1][2]. While it maintains a significant 392.9 months of working capital, the impact score reflects a balanced view of its direct service model and the medium confidence level in available performance data [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Irusa Waqf serves as a strategic endowment fund designed to ensure the long-term sustainability and operational continuity of Islamic Relief USA's humanitarian mission.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 80.1% dedicated to its core mission
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.06 per $1 raised
Robust financial stability with 392.9 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Established to maintain a permanent pool of capital, Irusa Waqf focuses on the WAQF IRUSA Endowment and specific distribution programs to support charitable objectives [1][2]. The organization reported a total revenue of $2,405,280, utilizing these funds to build a lasting financial foundation for ongoing sadaqah-eligible initiatives [2][3].

all_citations.quote: designed to ensure the long-term sustainability and operational continuity of Islamic Relief USA
Programs: WAQF IRUSA Endowment, Zakat Distribution, Fidya and Kaffara

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The charity earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and stability, though its impact score is tempered by the nature of endowment-based direct service.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization currently operates with a <cite id="15">minimal board size of 4 members</cite>, which is lower than the recommended size for organizations of this asset scale.
While the organization tracks outputs like <cite id="16">1,200 households served in the Philippines</cite>, it lacks <cite id="17">third-party impact evaluations</cite> to verify long-term outcomes.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization maintains audited financial statements and high transparency in its tax filings, though it lacks a full BBB Wise Giving Alliance accreditation.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's massive asset reserves mitigate immediate financial risks from revenue volatility.
risk_factors: <cite id="22">Revenue growth has declined by 28.3%</cite> over the last three years
<cite id="15">Board oversight is limited to 4 individuals</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="15">small board size</cite> and the <cite id="22">negative 3-year revenue CAGR of -28.3%</cite>, which may indicate fluctuating donor interest or asset transfers.

confidence.notes: Financial data is robust, but governance and impact evidence are areas of lower transparency.

alignment.explanation: The charity offers a <cite id="20">high Muslim donor fit</cite> by providing specialized vehicles for <cite id="9">sadaqah jariyah</cite>, fidya, and kaffara, serving <cite id="21">underserved populations</cite> globally.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further improved by <cite id="21">expanding services to geographies</cite> with even more limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Irusa Waqf due to its <cite id="13">strong asset base of over $15 million</cite> and its <cite id="18">100% independent board members</cite>. It has a <cite id="2">34-year track record</cite> of supporting the broader Islamic Relief network.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="15">expand its board size</cite> and publish a clear reserve-use policy to explain its <cite id="10">392.9 months of capital</cite>.

impact.explanation: The organization achieves a <cite id="19">cost per beneficiary of $8.68</cite>, which is considered exceptional for extreme poverty interventions. It maintains a <cite id="6">0.0% program ratio</cite> while operating a direct service delivery model.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="17">seeking external evaluation</cite> or tracking outcomes over at least three consecutive years to move beyond simple output metrics.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Internal output tracking with clear theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes endowment earnings to implementing partners for humanitarian projects

rich_narrative.headline: Irusa Waqf serves as a strategic endowment fund designed to ensure the long-term sustainability and operational continuity of Islamic Relief USA's humanitarian mission.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to establish a perpetual legacy of giving through an endowment model that ensures long-term support for Muslim humanitarian causes.
donor_motivations: Want to provide sadaqah jariyah that lasts beyond their lifetime
Value financial stability and long-term institutional planning
Prefer supporting an established name like Islamic Relief USA
Interested in specific religious giving like fidya and kaffara
giving_considerations: Donations are invested to generate long-term returns rather than immediate relief
The organization has a very high reserve ratio, which is typical for a Waqf
Revenue has shown significant volatility in recent years
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate, 100% pass-through emergency relief or those who require rigorous third-party impact evaluations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects clear output tracking (e.g., households served) and a documented theory of change, but lacks independent academic evaluation.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization invests capital to create a sustainable stream of income that funds long-term humanitarian interventions and emergency response capacity.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the endowment's distributions are effectively alleviating poverty and not just providing temporary aid.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As an endowment, the organization has unlimited capacity to absorb capital to increase its annual distribution amounts.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="10">massive 392-month reserve</cite> compared to the <cite id="23">peer program ratio median of 83.0%</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on humanitarian relief and size

similar_organizations.name: Abdul Sattar Edhi International Foundation

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates high fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="8">$0.06 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than many industry peers.
As an endowment-focused entity, it maintains <cite id="10">392.9 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer for long-term operational continuity.
Despite its investment-heavy model, it directs <cite id="6">0.0% of its expenses toward programs</cite> and charitable distributions.

strengths_deep_dive: Irusa Waqf's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and its role as a financial anchor for Islamic Relief USA. Founded in <cite id="2">1992</cite>, it has decades of experience managing endowment funds that support <cite id="11">orphan sponsorship</cite> and <cite id="12">flood relief beneficiaries</cite>. Its financial health is underscored by <cite id="13">total assets of $15,291,521</cite> against minimal <cite id="14">liabilities of only $32,742</cite>, representing a highly solvent and low-risk financial profile for long-term donors.
rich_narrative.summary: Irusa Waqf serves as a strategic endowment fund designed to ensure the <cite id="1">long-term sustainability and operational continuity</cite> of Islamic Relief USA's humanitarian mission. Established in <cite id="2">1992</cite>, the organization functions as a permanent pool of capital, investing donations to generate ongoing support for charitable objectives. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $1,912,900</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="4">contributions totaling $753,608</cite>. Unlike traditional relief models that spend all immediate donations, Irusa Waqf focuses on the <cite id="5">WAQF IRUSA Endowment</cite>, which supports long-term projects such as orphan sponsorship and sustainable development. The organization maintains a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 80.1%</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of its resources are directed toward its core mission of building a lasting financial foundation for humanitarian aid. Its operations are characterized by <cite id="7">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only <cite id="8">$0.08 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable support. This lean financial model allows the organization to maximize the value of sadaqah-eligible contributions, serving as a reliable vehicle for donors seeking to create a <cite id="9">sadaqah jariyah (perpetual charity)</cite> impact across international and domestic programs.
